name: Castro de As Muradellas - running round trip from the Santuario de la Tuiza
name_original: Castro de As Muradellas — running ida y vuelta desde el Santuario de la Tuiza
region_intro: Jogging routes around Lubián offer a diverse experience through mountainous terrain and river valleys in the Alta Sanabria sub-region of Zamora. The area is characterized by its varied elevations, with the Sierra Segundera and Sierra Gamoneda flanking the Tuela River valley. This geographical setting provides a range of running options, from challenging uphill paths to gentler riverside trails. Lubián's location within the rich natural landscapes of Alta Sanabria ensures runners are immersed in pristine nature, featuring ancient trees and lush greenery. Best jogging routes around Lubián The most popular jogging route is Bosque El Tejedelo Trail, a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail that takes 44 minutes to complete. This moderate route winds through an ancient yew forest, offering an atmospheric running experience. Another top favourite among local runners is Sotillo Waterfall and Lake Trail, a difficult 7.8 miles (12.6 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes and leads to the impressive Sotillo Waterfall and Laguna de Sotillo. Local runners also love the Chestnut Path and Hermisende Meadows — running circular through the Valley, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) trail leading through pastoral landscapes, often completed in about 56 minutes. Jogging around Lubián is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and ancient forests, with routes accessible within the Parque Natural del Lago de Sanabria y Alrededores. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails near Lubián?
A: The running routes around Lubián offer a diverse experience, characterized by mountainous terrain and river valleys. You'll find varying elevations, with paths winding through the Sierra Segundera and Sierra Gamoneda, as well as gentler trails along the Tuela River valley. This geographical setting provides options for both challenging ascents and more relaxed jogs.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: Yes, there are options for easier runs. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026503587/castro-de-as-muradellas-running-round-trip-from-the-santuario-de-la-tuiza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de As Muradellas - running round trip from the Santuario de la Tuiza&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route that offers historical interest and views of the Tuela River valley. The region's diverse topography ensures there are paths for various fitness levels.

Q: Can I find challenging running trails with significant elevation changes?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a more demanding run, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026652248/sotillo-waterfall-and-lake-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo Waterfall and Lake Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 7.8-mile path with significant elevation changes, leading to the impressive Sotillo Waterfall and Laguna de Sotillo. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20148581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Lake Sanabria loop from Ribadelago Viejo&lt;/a&gt;, which features substantial elevation gain.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by waterfalls or lakes?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026652248/sotillo-waterfall-and-lake-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo Waterfall and Lake Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, leading directly to the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4708534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and Laguna de Sotillo. You can also find routes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/872404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Serrada Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5451747" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Sanabria&lt;/a&gt;, offering beautiful waterside scenery for your run.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while running in Lubián?
A: Lubián's routes are rich in natural beauty. You can run through ancient forests like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3420090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tejedelo Forest&lt;/a&gt; on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026614893/bosque-el-tejedelo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque El Tejedelo Trail&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4708534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The region also offers stunning viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4954373" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Montesinho Hills&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7989709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Puente Porto reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, providing scenic rewards during your jog.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026623991/chestnut-path-and-hermisende-meadows-running-circular-through-the-valley" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chestnut Path and Hermisende Meadows — running circular through the Valley&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.8-mile circular trail that takes you through pastoral landscapes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20148581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Lake Sanabria loop from Ribadelago Viejo&lt;/a&gt;, offering a longer circular experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Lubián?
A: Lubián, situated in Alta Sanabria, boasts lush greenery that persists throughout the year, making it suitable for outdoor activities in many seasons. The clean air and pristine nature are consistent draws. However, for optimal conditions, spring and autumn generally offer milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes, ideal for running. Summer can also be pleasant, especially in the mornings or evenings.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Lubián?
A: The komoot community highly rates the running experience in Lubián, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the immersive natural environment, the variety of terrain from challenging mountain paths to serene river valleys, and the opportunity to discover ancient forests and impressive waterfalls. The region's clean air and tranquil atmosphere are frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural points of interest accessible via running routes?
A: Yes, you can combine your run with a visit to historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026503587/castro-de-as-muradellas-running-round-trip-from-the-santuario-de-la-tuiza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de As Muradellas - running round trip from the Santuario de la Tuiza&lt;/a&gt; leads to an ancient Asturian castro from the 3rd century BC, offering not only historical interest but also impressive views of the Tuela River valley.

Q: Are the running routes in Lubián suitable for families?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, the variety of routes means there are options. Easier paths like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026503587/castro-de-as-muradellas-running-round-trip-from-the-santuario-de-la-tuiza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de As Muradellas - running round trip from the Santuario de la Tuiza&lt;/a&gt;, with its lower elevation gain and shorter distance, could be suitable for families looking for a gentle jog or walk together. Always check the route details for distance and elevation to match your family's capabilities.

Q: Are there any running trails that are part of the Camino de Santiago?
A: Yes, Lubián is located on the historic Camino de Santiago. Specifically, it is the final municipality in Zamora on the Mozárabe-Sanabrés Way. This pilgrimage route provides well-established paths that can be utilized for jogging, connecting Lubián to a broader network of scenic trails and offering a unique historical context to your run.
tips:
Beautiful sanctuary in the heart of Sanabria Alta. Close to the highway, it is worth taking a detour to visit this spectacular large hermitage, which surely will not leave us indifferent.

Nearby, we will find a river with a fluvial beach area, and a hiking route to the Castro de As Muradellas. Or also the Tío mill.

The construction dates from the 18th century and two important pilgrimages are held (August 5 in honor of the Virgen de las Nieves and the last Sunday of September in honor of La Tuiza).

Picturesque bridge that crosses the Tuela river near the Tuiza Sanctuary and that brings us closer to the Tío Juan maquiler mill along a beautiful path between oaks and chestnut trees up to the
Castro de As Muradellas next to the river. The area is perfectly signposted with informative and indicative signs.

At this point we find ourselves in a meander on which the castro of "As Muradellas" sits, an Astur settlement from the 3rd century BC. C. (the Iron Age) where remains of defensive structures such as walls and moats are preserved. It also preserves remains of what was the town. In this enclave we can also enjoy magnificent views of the gorges and meanders formed by the Tuela river.
